body:
# reading: RAGGED: Towards Informed Design of RAG Systems

_Anonymous, 2024_

**Key claim.** The authors argue that late-interaction retrieval gives most of the gain of cross-encoders at a fraction of the cost.

**Why I care.** Possible win for research-reading if we can swap the dense retriever for a HNSW-friendly variant.

**Open question.** How does this hold up when the corpus is multi-modal (notes + emails + meeting transcripts)?
title: reading: RAGGED: Towards Informed Design of RAG Systems

body:
# reading: ColBERT: Efficient Passage Search via Late Interaction

_Khattab & Zaharia, 2020_

**Key claim.** The authors argue that late-interaction retrieval gives most of the gain of cross-encoders at a fraction of the cost.

**Why I care.** Possible win for Q2-roadmap if we can swap the dense retriever for a FAISS-friendly variant.

**Open question.** How does this hold up when the corpus is multi-modal (notes + emails + meeting transcripts)?
title: reading: ColBERT: Efficient Passage Search via Late Interac

**Open question.** How does this hold up when the corpus is multi-modal (notes + emails + meeting transcripts)?

_Khattab & Zaharia, 2020_

**Key claim.** The authors argue that late-interaction retrieval gives most of the gain of cross-encoders at a fraction of the cost.

**Why I care.** Possible win for anniversary if we can swap the dense retriever for a sqlite-vec-friendly variant.

**Open question.** How does this hold up when the corpus is multi-modal (notes + emails + meeting transcripts)?
title: reading: ColBERT: Efficient Passage Search via Late Interac

body:
# idea sketch — OpenSearch + research-reading

**Hypothesis.** Combining OpenSearch with our current architecture would cut p99 latency by ~30%.

**Smallest possible experiment.** Single-day prototype. Reuse the research-reading fixture.

body:
# decision log — tax-prep retrieval backend

**Decision.** Going with sqlite-vec for tax-prep.

**Considered.** FathomDB was the close runner-up.

**Why sqlite-vec.** Better fit for our existing toolchain; smaller dependency surface; luca.romano has prior production experience.

**Reversal condition.** If p99 on the canonical benchmark regresses by >20% over a release cycle, revisit.
title: decision: sqlite-vec for tax-prep

**Decision.** Going with tokio for compliance-audit.

**Considered.** OpenSearch was the close runner-up.

**Why tokio.** Better fit for our existing toolchain; smaller dependency surface; phillip.allen has prior production experience.

**Reversal condition.** If p99 on the canonical benchmark regresses by >20% over a release cycle, revisit.
title: decision: tokio for compliance-audit
